response.justification: Despite extensive full-stack and cross-platform experience, the candidate lacks demonstrable C++ expertise in industrial automation contexts, with no evidence of STL, multi-threading, message brokers, IEC 62443, or IoT protocols critical to the role; most recent work centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with only a brief, outdated C++ stint at National Instruments over 9 years ago.
strengths: Strong full-stack and cross-platform development experience across web, embedded, and mobile systems.
Proven track record in open-source contributions and technical mentorship through workshops and tooling.
Extensive background in distributed systems, event-driven architectures, and DevOps infrastructure.
concerns: No recent or substantial C++/STL work; core technical requirement of the role is severely underrepresented.
Lacks exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), MQTT, REST in automation contexts, or industrial protocols (OPC UA, Modbus).
Current role focuses on AI-agent tooling and web stacks, not on high-performance, low-latency on-prem/cloud industrial platforms.
